description_long: This object is a rustic stool made from a solid tree trunk, featuring a roughly cylindrical shape with a flat, sawed-off top surface suitable for sitting. The bark is mostly intact around the sides, displaying natural textures, knots, and crevices typical of aged wood. The top surface is weathered and slightly uneven, with visible growth rings and a few small patches of moss or lichen. The base is irregular, with some green moss at the bottom edges, suggesting it was cut from a living tree and possibly used outdoors. The stool has no visible legs, backrest, or footrest, relying on the solid trunk for support. Its natural, unrefined appearance makes it suitable for parks, gardens, or urban green spaces where organic aesthetics are desired.
description: A rustic, cylindrical wooden stool made from a solid tree trunk with intact bark and a flat, weathered top surface. Natural wood textures, knots, and moss are visible, giving it an organic, outdoor look.
description_view_0: The front view shows a flat, wide top with visible growth rings and a prominent bark texture on the side, including some rough, jagged areas.
description_view_1: This side view highlights a smoother section of bark with fewer knots and a more uniform texture, while the top remains flat and weathered.
description_view_2: This angle reveals a large knot in the bark and a patch of moss at the base, emphasizing the natural irregularities of the wood.
description_view_3: The rear view displays deep crevices and rough bark, with some green moss at the bottom and a slightly uneven top edge.
